# Open Bankruptcy Project

> Empirical research and free tools for bankruptcy court transparency.

The Open Bankruptcy Project is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that builds free, open-source screening tools analyzing 4.9 million federal bankruptcy cases across all 94 judicial districts. Our research has been cited by the Federal Rules Advisory Committee as Suggestion 26-BK-3.

## What We Do

- Screen 4,895,163 federal bankruptcy cases for statutory compliance failures
- Operate a 46-domain educational network covering every major bankruptcy statute
- Provide free discharge eligibility screening tools (Sections 1328(f), 727(a)(8), 109(g))
- Conduct empirical research on bankruptcy outcomes, attorney performance, and enforcement gaps
- Advocate for federal rules changes -- Suggestion 26-BK-3 accepted March 2026
- Publish 1,040+ pages of free educational content including Spanish-language resources
- Methodology validated by faculty at UC Berkeley School of Law

## Key Statistics

- 4,895,163 cases screened from Federal Judicial Center data (2008-2024)
- 94 federal judicial districts covered
- 391,951 prior filers identified with zero eligibility verification
- 46 educational domains in the network
- 1,040+ pages of free content
- $0 cost to users
- 26-BK-3 Federal Rules Advisory Committee citation
- 501(c)(3) nonprofit

## Our Network

### Screening & Research
- 1328f.com -- Discharge eligibility screener (Section 1328(f))
- 1328f.org -- Research platform with attorney analytics and district statistics

### Statute Guides
- 523a.org -- Nondischargeable debts (Section 523(a))
- 727a8.com -- Chapter 7 discharge bars (Section 727(a)(8))
- 109g.org -- Filing eligibility (Section 109(g))
- section1328.org -- Chapter 13 discharge (Section 1328)
- section1191.org -- Subchapter V discharge (Section 1191)
- section1192.org -- Subchapter V nondischargeable debts (Section 1192)
- section329.org -- Attorney fee disclosure (Section 329)
- dischargebar.org -- Comprehensive discharge bar guide

### Plain English Guides
- automaticstay.org -- The automatic stay (Section 362)
- 341meeting.org -- Meeting of creditors (Section 341)
- meanstest.org -- The means test (Section 707(b))
- relieffromstay.org -- Relief from stay motions
- dischargeinjunction.org / dischargeinjunction.com -- The discharge injunction (Section 524)
- codebtorstay.org -- The codebtor stay (Section 1301)

### Consumer Resources
- whatischapter7.com / whatischapter7.org -- Chapter 7 overview
- chapter13plan.org -- Understanding the Chapter 13 plan
- lienstripping.org -- Lien stripping in Chapter 13
- nondischargeable.org -- Guide to nondischargeable debts
- bankruptcyfreshstart.org -- Fresh start after bankruptcy
- reaffirmationagreement.org -- Reaffirmation agreements
- bankruptcytaxes.org -- Tax obligations in bankruptcy
- bankruptcyhardship.org -- Hardship discharge (Section 1328(b))
- voluntarypetition.org -- Filing the voluntary petition
- canifileagain.org -- Repeat filing eligibility calculator
- howtofilebankruptcy.org -- Step-by-step filing guide
- garnishedwages.org -- Wage garnishment and bankruptcy

### Pro Se & Advocacy
- prosedebtors.org -- Resources for pro se filers
- prosebankruptcy.org -- Pro se filing guide
- bankruptcymill.org / bankruptcymill.com -- High-volume practice identification
- bankruptcymalpractice.org -- Bankruptcy malpractice resources

### Data & Analysis
- dismissalrate.org -- Dismissal rate data by district and attorney
- dismissedbankruptcy.org -- Understanding bankruptcy dismissal
- serialfiler.org -- Serial filing patterns and analysis

## Pages

- https://openbankruptcyproject.org/ -- Homepage
- https://openbankruptcyproject.org/about.html -- About: mission, history, methodology, governance
- https://openbankruptcyproject.org/research.html -- Research: methodology, 26-BK-3, key findings, collaboration
- https://openbankruptcyproject.org/contact.html -- Contact: general, press, research collaboration
